annotation.personal_flag: May 25 into June: the tail of Mentawai's biggest swell window — Rifles and Bank Vaults at maximum expression, fewer boats than July, no pre-booked charter pressure.
annotation.raw_reasoning: May 25–Jun 3 in Mentawai (Kiwi, 2 stops via DOH+CGK, 19.8h outbound). Late May peak swell window narrowing to 10 days. Same quality surf conditions as the longer lastminute window, compressed. Crowd low: fewer boat operators in May vs June–August. Isolation 4. Surf quality 4: prime May swell period. €1,237 Kiwi vs €1,022 lastminute for similar window/route — higher price, shorter outbound travel (19.8h vs 40.8h). For 10 days on the water this is a reasonable trade. First baseline data point for MAD-PDG route.

annotation.personal_flag: Pre-peak Lombok: offshore winds established, Desert Point surfable daily, lineups half-empty — all of the conditions with none of the July pilgrim pack.
annotation.raw_reasoning: May 23–Jun 6 in Lombok (lastminute, 2 stops via EY/Garuda, 41h45m outbound — long haul). May: dry season fully established, SE trades offshore all morning. Desert Point reportedly surfable 70–80% of days in May. Selong Belanak consistent. Water 27°C. Crowd low: well before Jul 15–Aug 31 avoid window. June 1–6 sees swell strengthening as winter consolidates. Surf quality 3: building season, not peak power yet. Isolation 4: south Lombok structurally remote. At €994 lastminute this is the cheapest Lombok option — significant discount vs Kiwi options. 41h45m travel time is long but under 50h hard limit. No notable events or distortions.

notable_events: Mentawai peak season begins May–June: boat charters filling, resorts requiring advance booking
annotation.personal_flag: Late May Mentawai: historically the biggest swell month for rights — Macaronis, Rifles, Bank Vaults firing before the lineups fill with June-arrival boat charters.
annotation.raw_reasoning: May 23–Jun 6 in Mentawai (via PDG then ferry to Sipora/Siberut, ~3h crossing). May is Mentawai's historically biggest swell month for the right-hand breaks. June: excellent barrel consistency. Combined window captures both peaks. SE swell 3–8ft, N offshore winds. Crowd medium: boat charters arriving in volume for peak season, but early May/June sees fewer boats than July–August. Resorts and boats need advance booking. Isolation 4: archipelago geography forces distance from mainland crowds. Surf quality 4: top-tier waves, not quite peak consistency of July but historically biggest individual sessions. €1,022 lastminute, 2 stops via EY+Garuda, 40h45m — cheaper than Kiwi at €1,237. No events distorting.

notable_events: SW Monsoon onset expected early-to-mid June — El Nido northern island-hopping routes close for swells >1.5m from ~Jun 1
Coron is more sheltered from SW monsoon than El Nido — Jun 1–3 manageable at Coron
annotation.personal_flag: Last clear window before monsoon locks out northern Palawan — Coron's hidden lagoons and WWII wrecks nearly tourist-free, May mornings still crystal for snorkelling.
annotation.raw_reasoning: May 28–Jun 3 in Palawan. Late May is the final week of workable conditions before SW monsoon arrival. Temperatures 28–31°C, some afternoon showers possible (11 rainy days/month in May but mostly short downpours). El Nido northern boat routes (Linapacan, Dimancal) risk closure from early June. Coron is significantly more sheltered from the SW monsoon — remains navigable through most of June. Crowd low: off-peak, Palawan post-Easter crowd has cleared out, monsoon-adjacent months see minimal international visitors. Water visibility still excellent. Isolation 4: Coron's geographic spread means genuine solitude on most dive/snorkel sites. Surf quality null (maritime, not surf destination). Weather 3: manageable but transitional — mornings clear, afternoon risk from Jun 1. €1,341 via AUH+MNL, 27.1h outbound. Thin baseline — no prior MAD-PPS records in data.

annotation.personal_flag: Peak winter swell at Tofinho — consistent powerful barrels, NW offshore winds, empty lineups, whale sharks in the channel, and not another surf tourist in sight until August.
annotation.raw_reasoning: Jun 24–Jul 5 in Tofo. This is the heart of Mozambique's surf season: June and July are the best months, with consistent winter swells, most favourable winds (NW offshore), and longest clean windows. Tofinho Point firing reliably. Water temperature a comfortable 25–26°C despite 'winter' classification. Crowds extremely low: Tofo doesn't attract mass surf tourism the way Bali or Maldives does — lineups remain intimate even at peak season. 11 nights is a genuine surf trip. Weather 4: dry, cool, offshore. Air temp drops to 19–22°C at night, perfect for sleeping. Isolation 5. Surf quality 4: peak season reliability, solid head-to-overhead swell. €2,037 via DOH+MPM — above €1,500 sweet spot but within budget, and thin data (no prior records for this route).

notable_events: Mentawai peak season Jun–Aug: boat charters fully booked, camps at capacity — pre-booking 12 months ahead recommended
World Surf League events may coincide with prime windows (unconfirmed)
annotation.personal_flag: June 21–July 5 at Mentawai peak: Macaronis, Hollow Trees, and Rifles at absolute best — but book the boat charter NOW, resorts are full and lineups will have audience.
annotation.raw_reasoning: Jun 21–Jul 5 in Mentawai (Kiwi Window B, 2 stops via DOH+CGK, 22.1h). Peak season: consistent overhead to double-overhead swell, strongest SE swells of year, Hollow Trees (HTs) / Macaronis / Rifles world-class. Surf quality 5. Weather 5: dry season peak, offshore winds, 30°C water. Crowd high: peak season, all boats operating, lineups at capacity at famous breaks. Resorts fully booked 12 months in advance. Isolation 3: difficult to find uncrowded peaks at prime breaks without flexible boat route. €1,210 via DOH+CGK — cheapest Window B option, competitive for peak season but crowds are the cost. Traveller prefers low crowds — this window scores poorly on that axis.

notable_events: Moto GP Mandalika typically September — no conflict
Peak surf season Jun–Aug: lineups busier from Jun 25 onward, desert Point drawing serious surfers
annotation.personal_flag: Jun 23–Jul 5: Desert Point in its barrel window, consistent 4–8ft swell, offshore winds daily — 12 days before the Jul 15 avoid period begins and the crowds go exponential.
annotation.raw_reasoning: Jun 23–Jul 5 in Lombok. Full peak dry season: consistent SE groundswell 4–8ft, strong offshore winds all morning, Desert Point firing regularly (advanced/expert break — powerful tubes, shallow reef). Selong Belanak classic 3–5ft for intermediate surfers. Water 27°C, air 27–30°C, zero rain. Weather 5. Crowd medium: June is high season but window closes Jul 5, safely before the Jul 15–Aug 31 avoid period when international surfers flood the lineups. Isolation 4. Surf quality 5: peak performance window. €1,266 via DOH+CGK — Kiwi Window B, competitive for peak season. Price vs prior scan (May 8): €1,266 vs €1,314 observed previously — modest downward movement, moderate deal signal with building baseline.

notable_events: Madeira Flower Festival ends May 24 — entire stay (May 24→Jun 3) is post-festival
annotation.personal_flag: Direct €207 MAD-FNC departing the day the Flower Festival closes — land in a Funchal that's just exhaled: no festival prices, no parade crowds, levada trails to yourself for 10 days.
annotation.raw_reasoning: May 24–Jun 3 in Madeira. Direct Iberia/Vueling flight 2h40m at €207 — exceptional value. Flower Festival ends May 24 (same day as departure), meaning the entire 10-day stay is comfortably post-festival. Late May: 20–22°C, Madeira's statistically driest period, levadas at full flow after spring rains but trails uncrowded. June 1–3: early summer onset, still very manageable visitor numbers. Weather score 4. Crowd medium (shoulder, building slowly). Isolation 3. No distortion — €207 reflects structural route pricing for late May. Strong value signal vs prior scan (€189 on May 8 scan, now €207 = slight increase but consistent range).

annotation.personal_flag: Prime surf season opens at Tofinho Point — consistent winter E-SE swell, NW offshore winds, empty lineups, no tourist infrastructure to dilute the raw Mozambican coast experience.
annotation.raw_reasoning: May 29–Jun 3 in Tofo/Inhambane area. Mozambique surf season runs May–September, peak June–July. May 29 is precisely the season opening: consistent swell from E-SE, offshore winds NW-W establishing. Tofinho Point offers advanced right-hand point break. Tofo Beach softer peaks for all levels. Water 26°C, air 26°C, 8h sun/day. Very low crowds: May is pre-peak season, few international surf travelers yet (most arrive June–August). Isolation 5: no mass tourism, genuine African coast with fishing villages, basic infrastructure. Surf quality 3 (season establishing, not peak — June/July are significantly more consistent). Note: only 5 nights due to return June 3 — minimum viable surf trip. Malaria precautions essential. €1,880 for 2-stop Kiwi routing via LIS+MPM — somewhat expensive for 5 nights but first baseline data point for route.

annotation.personal_flag: May 25 into June: Desert Point transitioning from 'fires occasionally' to 'fires reliably' — same shoulder-season quiet, 10 days shorter travel than the lastminute option but nearly €400 more.
annotation.raw_reasoning: May 25–Jun 3 in Lombok (Kiwi, 2 stops via AUH+CGK, 23.8h outbound). Same seasonal window as May 23 lastminute option. Conditions identical: pre-peak dry season, Desert Point establishing, Selong Belanak groomed. Crowd low, isolation 4, surf quality 3. €1,349 Kiwi vs €994 lastminute — significant price premium for essentially the same route/window. The Kiwi option has shorter outbound (23.8h vs 41.8h) which is the main advantage. Deduplication note: different travel window start dates (May 25 vs May 23), so both are surfaced — normalize script correctly retains both since dedupe key includes start date.

notable_events: Atlantic Festival (fireworks over Funchal Bay) runs Jun 5–28 — final pyromusical night Jun 28 is 4 days into trip
Santos Populares street parties late June — active on arrival
July is Madeira's peak tourist month — accommodation premiums, busy beaches and trails
annotation.personal_flag: Trip opens with Atlantic Festival's last fireworks night (Jun 28) over Funchal Bay, then pivots to full Madeira summer: whale watching, Serra d'Água valley hikes, and stable 33°C sunshine through July 5.
annotation.raw_reasoning: Jun 24–Jul 5 in Madeira. Peak summer: temps 25–33°C, water 22–24°C, near-zero rainfall, maximum sunshine hours. Atlantic Festival ends Jun 28 (4 days into trip) — catches the finale pyromusical fireworks show. From Jul 1 full peak season: highest visitor numbers, trails and viewpoints at capacity, accommodation premium. Weather score 5: absolute best conditions. Crowd high: July is the busiest month. Isolation 2: genuinely difficult to escape crowds in standard Funchal/coastal areas. 1-stop via OPO at €216 — competitive for peak summer. Events are structural annual features, not distortion.

notable_events: Madeira Flower Festival ends May 24 — first day of trip marginally overlaps festival close
Post-festival window May 25 onward: Funchal returns to quiet shoulder mode through June 6
annotation.distortion_reason: TAP via LIS shows €305 vs €207 direct on Kiwi for similar window — 1-stop routing, not festival distortion; flag for visibility since first night lands during festival final hours
annotation.personal_flag: Arrive at Flower Festival's last exhale — Funchal still fragrant, then immediately quiets into perfect shoulder June hiking window through the 6th.
annotation.raw_reasoning: May 23–Jun 6 in Madeira via TAP/LIS. Flower Festival ends May 24; first night overlaps with closing ceremony atmosphere but accommodation is post-peak from May 25. Late May–early June: 20–23°C, low rainfall, levada trails at their seasonal best (25 Fontes, Caldeirão Verde). June builds tourist numbers gradually but early June is still notably less crowded than July. Weather score 4: stable, warm, hiking-optimal. Crowd medium: post-festival but entering early summer. Isolation 3: Funchal base, north coast day trips. 1-stop TAP via LIS at €305 — significantly more expensive than Kiwi direct option for similar window.
